courses.description: This course is devoted to the study of the management of marketing functions, the analysis of external forces affecting marketing decision making, the implementation and control of marketing activities, and an examination of the global impact of marketing. Course objectives include the development of students’ understanding of the fundamental concepts underlying the selection and assessment of markets and the development and delivery of products, an investigation of the role and contribution of marketing to the conduct of successful business operation and to society, and to develop student abilities in identifying marketing opportunities and viable marketing strategies.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. able to design and conduct marketing research to identify opportunities
2. able to formulate the appropriate marketing strategies in the current competitive environment
3. develop skills to work effectively in teams
4. improve communication skills in both written and oral marketing presentations
5. develop the competence for an insightful and knowledgeable marketer
courses.course_syllabus:
1. Orientation: What is Marketing all About: Chapter 1, 2, 3
2. Understanding Customers: Chapter 5
3. Marketing Research: Chapter 8
4. Segmentation, Targeting and Positioning: Chapter 9
5. Product Development: Chapter 10, 11
6. Branding and Service: Chapter 11
7. Pricing: Chapter 12
8. Distribution: Chapter 13, 14
9. Communication (I): Chapter 15, 16, 17
10. Communication (II) and course wrap up
11. Project Presentation
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Course Teaching Evaluation (CTE) will be done at the end of the course.
2. Collect information and feedback from students.
3. Assess the achievement of learning goals by reviewing external examiner reports.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Marketing Research
courses.description: A practical survey of contemporary systematic procedures and tools of research available to the marketing researcher, as well as the uses of such information in managerial decision making.
courses.enrollment_requirement: Prerequisite: MKTG2010 and DOTE2010 or DOTE2011 or STAT2006.
courses.learning_outcomes: After completion of the course, students should know what research alternatives exist, how to select the most effective method, and how to interpret and apply the results from data analyses.
courses.course_syllabus:
1. Introduction
2. Marketing Research Problem Definition and Approach
3. Research Design
4. Secondary Research
5. Scales
6. Questionnaire Design
7. Qualitative Studies
8. Descriptive Research
9. Experimental Design
10. Sampling
11. Data Entry, Verification & Validation
12. Summary Statistics
13. Hypothesis Testing
14. Crosstab
15. Regression
16. ANOVA
17. Discriminant Analysis
18. Cluster Analysis
19. Nonparametric Statistics.
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Course Teaching Evaluation (CTE) will be done at the end of the course.
2. Collect information and feedback from students.
3. Assess the achievement of learning goals by reviewing external examiner reports.
courses.required_readings: Basic Marketing Research, A Decision-Making Approach, 3rd ed. by Naresh K. Malhotra and Mark Peterson, Prentice Hall, 2006.
courses.recommended_readings:
Buzzell, R. D. 1963 "Is Marketing a Science?" Harvard Business Review, Vol. 41, Jan-Feb.

Andreason, A.R (1985), "Backward marketing research", Harvard Business Review, Vol. 63 pp.176, 180, 182.

Stevens, S. S. 1946 "On the Theory of Scales of Measurement;" Science, Vol. 103, p.p. 677 -80. Reprinted in Mehrens, W.A. and Ebel, R.L., (eds), 1967.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Consumer Behaviour
courses.description: This course is focused on the behavioural concepts and theories that have been found useful for understanding consumer behaviour and their potential applications in the development of marketing strategy. Concepts and theories in the social sciences, particularly social psychology, cognitive psychology, sociology and cultural anthropology will be emphasized throughout this course with special reference to selected consumer topics. These topics include attitude formation and change, information processing, learning, group influences, choice behaviour and cross-cultural consumer behaviour.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Appreciate their own behaviors by learning about the relevance of consumer psychology principles in the consumer decision-making process.
2. Have a basic understanding of the major consumer behavior theories and their implications for marketing practices.
3. Develop a critical mindset in analyzing how influences from environment and the consumers themselves contribute to the strategic choice of marketing program.
courses.course_syllabus:
1. What is consumer behavior?
2. The \*Process\* – Consumer decision making process
3. The \*Symbolic meanings\* – Personality, self image, and consumer behaviors
4. The \*Symbolic meanings\* – Cultural influences on consumer behaviors
5. The \*Black Box\* – Consumer attention & perceptions
6. The \*Black Box\* – Consumer learning and memory
7. The \*Black Box\* – Consumer attitudes
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Course Teaching Evaluation (CTE) will be done at the end of the course.
2. Collect information and feedback from students.
3. Assess the achievement of learning goals by reviewing external examiner reports.
courses.required_readings:
1. Solomon, Michael R. Consumer behavior: Buying, having, and being (9th Ed.). NY: Prentice Hall.
2. R#1 Kahneman, D. & Tversky, A. (1984). Choices, values, and frames. American Psychologist, 39(4), 341-350.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Integrated Marketing Communication
courses.description: This course is designed to provide students with a theoretical and practical appreciation of the role of "integrated marketing communication" (IMC) in today's business environment. IMC differs from traditional advertising and promotion programmes by using zero-based planning, data-driven communication and brand touch points. The programme focuses on using strategic mix of advertising, sales promotion, public relations, event marketing and direct response promotions along with mass and two-way communication.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. To understand the key issues, theories, strategies and tactics associated with integrated marketing communication decisions.
2. To develop an understanding of the psychological principles in consumer behavior that facilitates the development of marketing communication programs.
3. To be able to evaluate advertising, sales and promotion, and public relations programs.
4. To be able to develop an integrated communication program incorporating various elements of the promotional mix.
courses.course_syllabus:
1. IMC: The Big Picture
2. IMC Intro/Target Market/Positioning
3. Establishing IMC Objectives
4. Perspectives on Consumer Behavior
5. The Communication Process
6. Creative Strategy
7. Media Strategy
8. Sales and Promotion
9. Public Relations
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Course Teaching Evaluation (CTE) will be done at the end of the course.
2. Collect information and feedback from students.
3. Assess the achievement of learning goals by reviewing external examiner reports.
courses.required_readings: George Belch and Michael A. Belch, Advertising and Promotion: An Integrated Marketing Communications Perspective (8th Ed.) McGraw-Hill.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Global Marketing Management
courses.description: This course is designed to expose students to the complexities and challenges related to international marketing activities. Topics include an analysis of the underlying forces in international trade, opportunities and threats in the world market, estimation of market potential, formulation of multinational marketing strategies, organization and coordination of global marketing activities.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
Prerequisites: 
1. MGNT2510 or MGNT2511; and 
2. MKTG2010.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Appreciate the complexities and challenges related to global marketing
2. Develop an understanding of the impact of macro-environmental factors (i.e., opportunities and threats) on global marketing activities
3. Be able to analyze the underlying forces in international trade and estimate market potential
4. Understand the planning, implementation and control of global strategies
courses.course_syllabus:
1. Introduction to global marketing
2. The global economic environment
3. The political, legal, and regulatory environments
4. Global marketing information systems and research
5. Entry and expansion strategies
6. Competitive analysis and strategy
7. Pricing decisions
8. Global advertising
9. Global e.marketing
10. The future of global marketing
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Course Teaching Evaluation (CTE) will be done at the end of the course.
2. Collect information and feedback from students.
3. Assess the achievement of learning goals by reviewing external examiner reports.
courses.required_readings: Conceptual materials will be presented through lectures, class discussions, and case analyses. Participants are expected to preview all materials (PDF files and cases) prior to the associated sessions, and to actively participate in all discussions.
courses.recommended_readings: Warren J. Keegan, Global Marketing Management, Seventh Edition, Prentice-Hall, 2002.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Big Data Strategy in Marketing
courses.description: This course introduces big data marketing strategy to undergraduate students. It introduces (1) what role big data plays in marketing strategy, (2) what marketing problems can be tackled by big data, (3) how is the marketing strategy different by using big data compared with traditional marketing analysis, and (4) how to apply the basic big data technique to those marketing problems. As it is an introduction course, the focus is the applications of big data in the marketing area rather than advanced big data techniques.
courses.learning_outcomes: This course aims to help students understand the role of big data in marketing strategy, understand the difference between traditional marketing analysis and the new big data strategy in marketing, and develop very basic skills to apply big data strategy to marketing.
courses.course_syllabus:
- The Age of Big Data

- Key Uses of Big Data in Marketing

- Sourcing and Collecting Data, Social Media and User-Generated Content

- Web Scraping

- Data Mining

- Data Visualization

- Simple Linear Regression, Logistic Regression

- Web Search Data, Clickstream Data

2. Collect information and feedback from students.

3. Assess the achievement of learning goals by reviewing external examiner reports.

Reading materials will be assigned in advance. Students are expected to have read and analyzed the required materials for each class prior to attending the class.

courses.description: This course reviews the important concepts in service marketing. It addresses the issues involved in the effective marketing of services taking into account the unique characteristics of services. The issues which are covered include: the interactive nature of the service provider, the consumer and the physical environment in the service provision process; the importance to maintain high quality; and special problems related to the growth and internationalization of services.
courses.learning_outcomes:
The purpose of this course is to provide students with experience in making marketing decision in a variety of service firms. Specific learning outcomes include:

(a) Understanding of services and ability to identify key strategic issues in the marketing of services as distinct from the marketing of goods,

(b) Awareness of the latest concepts, tools and techniques in marketing of services and their theoretical underpinning, and

(c) Experiences in tackling real-life service marketing problems by applying (a) and (b).
courses.course_syllabus:
I. Services and Services Marketing

a) Why services marketing:
Importance of the Service Sector
b) What are services:
The Servuction System
c) Services characteristics and challenges

II. External Strategies

(a) Brand Building, Communication and Pricing
(b) Customer Retention and Service Recovery
(c) Service Quality: Conceptualization and Measurement

III. Internal Strategies

(a) Balancing marketing and operations
(b) Balancing marketing and HRM
(c) Building service culture
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Course Teaching Evaluation (CTE) will be done at the end of the course.
2. Collect information and feedback from students.
3. Assess the achievement of learning goals by reviewing external examiner reports.
courses.required_readings: Christopher Lovelock, Jochen Wirtz and Patricia Chew, Essentials of Services Marketing, Prentice Hall, 2009.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Strategic Marketing
courses.description: The purpose of this course is to reinforce and build upon previous studies of marketing and develop a comprehensive and integrated framework for the direction and management of the marketing function in a company. The approach of this course is broad, with marketing examined from the viewpoint of the enterprise rather than through business perspective. While this course necessarily includes reference to strategic management issues, major emphasis is placed upon the distinctive conceptual perspectives that are wholly within the domain of the firm’s marketing strategy. This course will build skills in such areas as: market opportunity analysis, the assessment of competitive advantages, forecasting patterns of market evolution, and developing marketing strategies consistent with these assessments. In addition, through case studies and other means, understanding is enhanced of the methods and concepts of marketing strategy analysis and how these can be applied in practice.
courses.learning_outcomes: Upon completion of this course, students are expected to have adequate knowledge and technical skills on strategic marketing planning, implementation, evaluation and control. Most of all, this course should enable students to perform as an innovative marketing manager who can apply marketing concepts and theories into the real business world.
courses.course_syllabus:
1. Imperative for Market –Driven Strategy
2. Markets & Competitive Space
3. STP- Segmentation, Targeting & Positioning
4. Strategic Brand Management
5. New Products and Product –Life-Cycle
6. Promotion, Advertising and Sales Promotion Strategies (IMC)
7. Marketing Mix Strategy
8. Effective Implementation of Marketing Strategy
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Course Teaching Evaluation (CTE) will be done at the end of the course.
2. Collect information and feedback from students.
3. Assess the achievement of learning goals by reviewing external examiner reports.
courses.required_readings: Cravens, D.W. & Nigel, F., “Strategic Marketing”, McGraw Hill (9th Edition)

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Marketing in China
courses.description: The major objective of this course is to prepare students to become a good decision maker in China marketing. Students will become aware of the strategies, skills and attitudes that are essential for effective and efficient marketing practices in China. Topics include: analysing the marketing environment in China; understanding Chinese consumer behaviour; conducting marketing research in China; developing and implementing marketing strategies in China.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Have a good knowledge of the social, economic, and political environment surrounding the Chinese market
2. Have a good understanding of the Chinese consumer characteristics and behavior patterns.
3. Be familiar with the unique characteristics of marketing mix management in China
4. Understand the nature, and learn to implement the winning strategies, of marketing competition between local and international companies in the Chinese market.
courses.course_syllabus:
1. Introduction
2. Understanding the Chinese Consumers
3. Advertising in China
4. Entering the Chinese Market
5. Production & Logistics & Service in China
6. Branding in China
7. Competition in the Chinese Market

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Digital Marketing
courses.description: The Internet is having a profound effect on the conduct of marketing as we move towards the new millennium. The Internet presents a fundamentally different environment for marketing, and new paradigms will have to be developed to take account of marketing activities in the electronic age. This course focuses primarily on the impact of the Internet on marketing, marketing research with Internet, consumer behaviour on the Internet, and marketing strategies in the Internet age.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. understand how the information revolution has fundamentally changed the world and the new marketing and business environment that we are facing,
2. appreciate the power of internet as a fast growing and engaging communication platform,
3. understand the basic skills of deploying internet in an effective manner.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Marketing Analytics
courses.description: Marketing analytics allow firms to measure, manage, and analyze marketing performance in order to maximize its effectiveness and return on investment. Advancements in collecting, storing, and analyzing information have created new business opportunities. This course “Marketing Analytics” introduces basic ideas of turning marketing data into prescriptive recommendations for the marketing execution process to maximize firms' strategic value. We will discuss how to use marketing data to answer marketing questions and how to analyze marketing data using empirical marketing techniques.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Marketing analytics involves not only techniques such as computer science, information system, statistics but also behavior aspects on consumers such as marketing. It includes key principles of such areas. Students will be able to master concepts, theories and insights of marketing data and modes.
2. Marketing data analytics is heavily technology driven. After taking this course, students will acquire techniques to statistically analyze marketing data.
3. This course helps students design and develop marketing strategies based on customer data analysis (e.g. customer value analysis), product data analysis (e.g., marketing mix models) and digital data analysis (e.g., social media analysis, search engine optimization). Students will be able to apply the analysis skills to marketing problems and measure marketing ROI based on data analysis.
courses.course_syllabus:
- Introduction
- Data-Driven Marketing
- Data Management
- Methodology Review
- Customer Analytics
- Marketing Mix Model
- Panel Data Analysis
- Pricing Analysis
- Sales Prediction
- Conjoint Analysis
- Big Data Marketing
- Search Engine& Mobile Marketing
- Social Network Marketing
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Course Teaching Evaluation (CTE) will be done at the end of the course.
2. Collect information and feedback from students.
3. Assess the achievement of learning goals by reviewing external examiner reports.
courses.required_readings: There is no required text book for this course. It is because the trend of data-driven marketing is moving so fast and many text books are already out-dated. Instead, students are required to read additional articles, cases etc to keep up with the latest issues and practices of database marketing.
courses.recommended_readings: We will discuss cases, articles and reports from time to time. These readings will be listed in the schedule. Students are expected to have read and analyzed the materials before coming to the class. In case one did not read them and cannot participate in-class discussions, s/he will lose the points assigned for the participation.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Strategic Brand Management
courses.description: One of the most important assets of any business is its brands. As industries become increasingly competitive, it is clear that strong brand-building skills are needed for every business to survive and prosper. The Strategic Brand Management course is designed to provide conceptual background as well as practical approaches to manage brands as a strategic asset as well as a primary source of competitive advantage. Topics include brand creation, brand building, managing brand equity, and revitalizing brands.
courses.learning_outcomes:
1. Understanding the fundamental concepts in strategic band management.
2. Effective communication skill.
3. Demonstration of analytical and problem solving skills.
4. Effective team-building and collaborative skills.
5. Ability to analyse business problems from an international and cross-cultural perspective.
6. Ability to think creatively and critically.

1. Strategic Marketing: A Vision and Decision for Marketing Actions
2. What is a Brand?
3. The Top 100 Global Brands
4. The Meanings of Brands
5. Managing Brand Equity
6. Brand Management: A Strategic Process Model
7. Brand Positioning
8. Communications for Brand Management
9. Brand Revitalization
10. Brand Crisis Management
11. Brand Protection

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: New Product Management
courses.description: The new product market is growing fast in the past few years. On one hand, it gives marketers a greater opportunity to approach consumers directly, customize products/services for different groups of consumers, and eventually become more profitable. On the other hand, new product market is characterized by growth patterns exhibiting a high degree of uncertainty that makes them exceptionally difficult to predict and forecast. This course will introduce you theories (e.g., diffusion theory) and best practice in new product management. Moreover, it will enable you to use diffusion model to predict the sales of new products and analyze customer life time value in this market. This course will also teach you how to make comprehensive marketing plans in the new product market.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students are expected to learn
 what are the characteristics of new product market
 what factors in Diffusion Model to influence customers’ demand
 how to predict sales at new product market using Diffusion Model and Optimization tools
 how to calculate customer lifetime value (CLV)
 what is product life cycle (PLC) and how does marketing strategies differ across stages of PLC
 how to do marketing research in new product market
 how to develop marketing plans in new product market
courses.course_syllabus:
1. Introduction to New Product Management
2. Creativity and New Product Concept
3. New Product Evaluation
4. Understand Diffusion of New Products and Diffusion Model
5. Product Life Cycle (PLC)
6. Predict and Accelerate Sales of New Products
7. Launch of New Products
8. Prediction of Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)
9. Marketing Research in New Product Management
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Course Teaching Evaluation (CTE) will be done at the end of the course.
2. Collect information and feedback from students.
3. Assess the achievement of learning goals by reviewing external examiner repots.
courses.required_readings:
For background of new product management:
Crawford, Merle C. and C. Anthony Di Benedetto, New Products Management, 11th edition, McGraw-Hill/Irwin Higher Education.

For quantitative models: Muller, Eitan, Renana Peres, Vijay Mahajan (2009), Innovation Diffusion and New Product Growth, Marketing Science Institute, Cambridge, MA.
courses.recommended_readings: Moore, Geoffrey A., Crossing the Chasm: Marketing and Selling High-Tech Products to Mainstream Customers, HarperBusiness.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Pricing
courses.description: Pricing is one of the most important marketing decisions because prices, on one hand, reflects the values a firm provides to its customers, and on the other hand, influences the profits a firm can earn. Therefore this course aims to introduce the basic concepts and techniques in pricing decisions. It introduces an integrated framework, which is structured around marketing's three C’s: costs, customers and competitors, for assessing and formulating profitable pricing strategies. It also introduces basic analytical tools which are essential for the understanding of both pricing decision and its value creation process from a managerial perspective. The applications of those analytical tools in marketing will also be discussed.
courses.learning_outcomes:
- Understand the value-based framework in formulating profitable pricing strategies
- Apply pricing strategies and tactics in dynamic business environment
courses.course_syllabus:
- Introduction (Value and Pricing), Customer Value and EVC
- Elasticity Analysis and Pricing, Demand and Elasticity Estimation using Regression
- Application of EVC, Conjoint Analysis for EVC and Pricing
- Costs for Pricing Decisions, Competition and Strategic Price Response
- Competitive Response and Price Leadership, Targeted Pricing: Targeting and Micromarketing
- Product Line Pricing Strategy and Tactics, NL Pricing Strategy
- Managing a Product Line: Centralized versus Decentralized Pricing, Bundling and Metered Pricing Strategy
- Channel Pricing Strategy, Customer Lifetime Value and Pricing
- Lifetime Value Pricing and Metering, Temporary Discounts, Hi-Lo Pricing Strategy, Skimming Strategy Experimentation and Mark-Downs
- Hi-Lo versus Every Day Fair Pricing
courses.feedback_evaluation:
1. Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course
2. Information and feedback will be gathered from students
courses.required_readings: Required case readings will be posted on the course website in advance of the respective in-class discussion.
courses.recommended_readings: Nagle, Muller, The Strategy and Tactics of Pricing, Sixth Edition, Routledge, 2017.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Machine Learning and AI Practices in Marketing
courses.description: Machine learning, with its capability of analyzing big databases for patterns and insights, is quickly altering the future landscape of marketing. This course uses real business cases from tech companies and aims at providing students with a basic understanding on how machine learning can assist modern marketing decisions. Topics such as the following will be covered: 1) unsupervised learning and recommendation systems; 2) supervised learning and churn rate prediction; 3) A/B testing and the optimization of advertising offers; 4) natural language processing and the analysis of user generated content. Deep learning and artificial intelligence will also be discussed.
courses.learning_outcomes: In this course, we will learn machine learning through real world examples and business cases from tech companies. Students will gain hands-on experience on how to use machine learning to derive business insights and assist marketing decisions. We will use tools such as Python and Jupyter notebook as the main device to clean, visualize, analyze data and finally adopt machine learning models.
courses.course_syllabus:
1. A short introduction to machine learning and programming language.
2. Unsupervised learning: clustering; Practice in marketing: Recommendation systems; Examples: Netflix film recommendation.
3. Supervised learning: regression; Practice in marketing: Churn rate prediction, Customer life-time value; Examples: Lyft churn rate prediction.
4. A/B testing; Practice in marketing: Optimizing advertising offers; Examples: Pricing at Amazon.
5. Natural language processing; Practice in marketing: Analysis of user generated content; Examples: Yelp reviews.
courses.feedback_evaluation:

courses.course_syllabus: To survive and develop in competitive marketplace, marketing managers need to scan marketing environment, formulate effective marketing strategy tailored to the specifics of the market and establish efficient marketing system. This course, by theory introduction and case study analysis, is to achieve the following objectives: 1) enabling the students to learn the core marketing concepts, build up integrated marketing analysis framework, and develop their skills and ability in marketing decision-making; 2) enabling the students to identify the role of marketing in the firms, understand the relationship between marketing and other functions of firms; 3) enabling the students to understand the leading theories and practices of marketing; and 4) enabling the students to be familiar with and comply with the moral rules. The course will first analyze opportunities and environment in the market and explore customers value, including customer needs forecast, customer behavior analysis and competitor behavior analysis of the market. It will then discuss marketing strategy for market development in terms of market segments, target market and market positioning, so as to identify and choose customer value. In the end, the course will discuss how to establish effective 4P portfolio (products, pricing, place and promotion) strategy and marketing system in line with the marketing strategy, with an aim to create, provide and communicate customer value.

courses.description: One of the most important assets of any business is tangible: its brands. As industries become increasingly competitive, it is clear that strong brand-building skills are needed to survive and prosper. The Strategic Brand Management course is designed to provide conceptual background as well as practical approaches (i.e., relevant research techniques) to manage brands as a strategic asset as well as a primary source of competitive advantage. Topics include new product development process, brand creation, brand building, measuring/monitoring brand equity, and revitalizing brands. Although brands are one of the most important assets for any business, it is found that managers generally do not have very clear idea how to successfully manage their brands. Far too few managers really understand the concept of brand equity and how it must be implemented. In other words, managers need to learn the basic brand building concepts as well as the research techniques that can be used to collect data for brand planning. The objective of this course is to provide students with understanding regarding brand building concepts as well as to equip students with research techniques that can be used to build and manage a successful brand.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Business Negotiation
courses.description: This course introduces current research and thought on the negotiation process, and provides opportunities for students to apply the concepts and skills in simulations of actual negotiations. Strategic lessons are learned from different actual cases. The objective is to enable students to appreciate the art and science of negotiation, to understand how to resolve conflicts and derive the most out of bargaining.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MBA, or JD/MBA Double Degree Programme; or EMBA; or EMBA (Chinese); or MSc Marketing; or MBA in Finance; or MBA in Technology and Innovation
courses.learning_outcomes:
Negotiation is the art and science of creating agreements between two or more parties. This course is designed to explore both parts. First, students will be exposed to different negotiation theories (the science). Using these theories as guides, we will then discuss how to come up with useful negotiation strategies and tactics. Second, students will develop and sharpen negotiation skill by actually negotiating with other students in realistic settings (the art).

This course is designed to complement the skills learned in other courses at CUHK. A basic premise of the course is that analytical skills are vital to discovery of optimal solutions to problems and that business people need a broad array of negotiation skills to get those solutions accepted and implemented. The course will allow students the opportunity to develop those negotiation skills through direct experience and to understand negotiation in useful analytic frameworks.
courses.course_syllabus:
The course will build cumulatively from (seemingly) simple negotiations to those of greater complexity. Structured negotiation exercises are used to isolate and emphasize specific analytic points and essential skills. Cases and readings should help you integrate the analysis and develop intuition about related, possibly more personally relevant situations. We will cover the following topics:

Distributive Negotiation (Value Claiming)

We begin by focusing on situations in which each party attempts to enlarge his or her share of what is taken to be a fixed pie. We will introduce basic concepts and tactics, with a special emphasis on the cognitive aspects of negotiation.

Multiple Parties/Multiple Issues

In the classes that follow, we study multiparty negotiations and explore how the dynamics of these negotiations differ from two party settings. In addition to dealing with multiple issues, we examine the strategic and tactical issues that are essential to any sophisticated building (or destroying) of coalitions.

Integrative Negotiation (Value Creating)

We will put it all together by exploring negotiations that contain elements of both cooperation and competition; that is, cases where the parties can “enlarge the pie” by creating joint gains. We identify the potential sources of joint gains – and why they are often not fully captured. We also analyze the tension between cooperative moves to create value and competitive moves to claim it. This tension is fundamental to negotiation and affects virtually every tactical and strategic choice.

courses.description:
This course is about reshaping marketing and communication strategy to promote long-term wellbeing for people and planet. Transformation of a company with positive impacts to the world will require a realignment of its purpose and role. It will involve rewiring how marketing is planned and practiced – including but not limited to the deployment of the skills and competencies of marketers and creatives in service of sustainability.

This course will use lecture and class exercises, as well as case studies of brands engaging in sustainable marketing to examine key issues, challenges and opportunities. You will gain a deeper understanding of sustainable marketing and branding. You will learn how a sustainable brand can be built and strengthened. You will also develop a marketing roadmap and communication plan with sustainable means and ends that stakeholders will value. Overall, you will acquire the know-how in identifying and conveying corporate sustainability purpose and initiatives inside and outside the company.

courses.title: Practicum in Strategic Marketing
courses.description: This course adopts an applied approach that integrates marketing concepts and analytics with hands-on experience in a real-world business setting. As a practicum course in strategic marketing, students will identify the marketing challenge, develop the framework for the project, gather the necessary information and data, undertake the necessary marketing analysis, and summarize their findings and recommendations in a final report. Students will work in teams, under the supervision of the professor, to conduct research and consulting studies for the client company, will learn how to apply the marketing knowledge they have acquired to tackle the marketing challenges and problems in real business world.
courses.course_syllabus:
* Introduction of Consulting Business and Marketing Strategy
* Problem Identification in Marketing Consulting
* Luxury marketing strategy
* Consulting Proposal Professional seminar/ project meetings
* Marketing strategy for the consulting project
* Presentation skills and Q&A Skills
courses.feedback_evaluation: Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course

* 3. Bernard Garrette, Corey Phelps and Olivier Sibony (2018), **Cracked it! How to solve big problems and sell solutions like top strategy consultants,** Palgrave, Macmillan.*

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: e-Platform Entrepreneurship
courses.description: This course empowers postgraduate business classmates with the practical digital marketing knowledge and the appropriate entrepreneurial mindset required to thrive in the dynamic digital marketplace. This course explores the intersection of technology and business, focusing on creating, managing, and growing online ventures managed by would-be entrepreneurs or future leaders who may contribute to the success of a digital enterprise.
courses.learning_outcomes:
On successful completion of this course, classmates should be able to:

1. Design a comprehensive e-commerce business plan.
2. Evaluate various digital marketing channels and select appropriate strategies for different business models.
3. Analyse case studies of successful e-commerce ventures to identify best practices.
4. Utilise tools for measuring performance and optimising online operations.
courses.course_syllabus:
This course aims to:

1. **Develop Entrepreneurial Mindsets**: Foster critical thinking and problem-solving abilities necessary for identifying opportunities in the e-commerce landscape.
2. **Understand Digital Business Models**: Analyse various e-commerce business models and their implications for entry, growth and exit strategies.
3. **Implement Digital Strategies**: Learn to create effective online marketing strategies, optimise user experience, utilise data analytics and ultimately enhance the conversion rate.
4. **Practical Application**: Through case studies and project-based learning, classmates will apply theoretical concepts to real-world scenarios, enabling them to develop actionable business plans.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course.
courses.required_readings:
Croxen-John, D., & van, T. J. (2017). E-commerce website optimization: Why 95% of your website visitors don't buy, and what you can do about it. Kogan Page, Limited.

Williams, S. (2022). FT Guide to Business start up 2021-2023 (Thirty-Second edition.). Pearson Education Limited.
courses.recommended_readings: To be assigned by course teacher

courses.title: Advanced Seminar in Marketing - Behavioral Studies I
courses.description: This seminar covers major consumer information processing related issues in behavioral marketing. It is focused on the selected current theory and research in areas of contemporary importance and relevance to consumer behavior. Topics will vary over course offerings but may include learning and memory, attitude and persuasion, mood and emotions, etc.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Special Topics in Behavioural Marketing
courses.description: This seminar course examines advanced concepts within the field of behavioural marketing, specifically focusing on the intricacies of consumer behaviour and decision-making processes. Topics will vary over course offerings but can include a comprehensive exploration of cognitive biases, emotional triggers, self-perceptions, motivations and other psychological factors that influence consumer perception, preference formation and choices. The course may also investigate methodological aspects, covering topics such as designing and conducting lab and field experiments to gain practical insights into consumer behaviour. Through a critical analysis of cutting-edge research and methodologies, students gain a deep understanding of the evolving landscape of behavioural marketing. This seminar encourages active engagement with emerging trends, fostering a dynamic and intellectually stimulating experience for students.
courses.learning_outcomes: After completing the course, students will have a deeper knowledge of state-of-the-art behavioural marketing theories and tools specialized in the field of marketing and its applications.
courses.course_syllabus: Topics will vary over course offerings but can include the exploration of cognitive biases, emotional triggers, self-perceptions, motivations and other psychological factors that influence consumer perception, preference formation and choices. The course may also investigate methodological aspects, covering topics such as designing and conducting lab and field experiments to gain practical insights into consumer behaviour.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Academic Communication
courses.description: This course aims at equipping students with the essential skills to effectively produce, present, and critique scholarly work in business field. Through a structured exploration of academic writing, peer review, research presentations, and job market strategies, students will learn to craft clear, persuasive arguments; navigate the publication process; and communicate research to diverse audiences. The course combines lectures, workshops, and peer feedback to bridge the gap between research rigor and communicative impact.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MPhil-PhD Accountancy; or MPhil-PhD Business Administration; or MPhil-PhD Decisions, Operations and Technology; or MPhil-PhD Finance; or MPhil-PhD Marketing; or MPhil-PhD Management
courses.learning_outcomes:
• First, the course will acquaint students with guidelines that will help them create well-crafted academic communication.
• Second, it will give students opportunities to practice their communication skills and to receive extensive feedback from peers and from me. Students will write and revise an article manuscript, give a short presentation, write a manuscript peer review, and engage in various other communication exercises. The article and talk, which are the major written assignments of the course, will be based on material from their own doctoral studies.
• Third, the course will provide an opportunity for students to learn about professional norms for a range of activities that surround the academic enterprise, including, for example, the scholarly publication process and the job search process.
courses.course_syllabus:
Lecture 1:
Course overview.
Lecture on oral presentations: strategy, structure, visual aids, delivery

Lecture 2:
Workshop of oral presentations: each student gives a short oral presentation and gets feedback

Lecture 3:
Workshop of oral presentations: each student gives a revised oral presentation
Lecture & discussion on academic writing:
• Language
• Abstract
• Introduction
• Literature Review
• Method
• Results: Tables and Figures
• Conclusion and discussion

Lecture 4:
Workshop of academic writing: each student submits a short manuscript (introduction + tables/figures + one section of the paper), and gets feedback

Lecture 5:
Workshop of academic writing: each student submits a revised manuscript
Lecture & discussion on review process:
• how to write referee reports
• go through sample referee reports together

Lecture 6:
Workshop of review process: each student submits a referee report and gets feedback

Lecture 7:
Lecture & discussion on how to write response letters, go through sample response letters together

Lecture 8:
Lecture & discussion on job market:
• Material preparation (CV, research statements, teaching statements)
• Interviews
• Flyouts and job talks

Lecture 9:
Lecture & discussion on teaching

Lecture 10:
Lecture & discussion on academic project management:
• How to manage the relationship with your advisors & coauthors
• How to manage project folders and files

Lecture 11:
Course wrap-up
courses.feedback_evaluation: Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course.

courses.recommended_readings: To be assigned by course teacher.

courses.subject: MKTG
courses.title: Advanced Seminar in Marketing- Empirical Models
courses.description: This seminar course emphasizes on empirical quantitative models of marketing research. It will cover a wide range of topics including consumer choice modeling, Bayesian modeling, structural modeling, dynamic modeling, data mining, and research advances in marketing. The course will also introduce some basic analytical tools from areas of network analysis, economics, and statistics.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Advanced Seminar in Marketing-Empirical Models aims to explore empirical quantitative modeling techniques in marketing studies. By the end of this course, students should be able to master basic empirical quantitative models, understand how to implement them as well as how to interpret results. Understanding how to apply empirical data analysis tools in marketing research questions is the essence for conducting rigorous empirical
quantitative marketing research.
